message:
test(storage): 4 metamorphic relations for FTS5 lexical search

Adds tests/storage_search_metamorphic.rs exercising four input/output
relations that must hold for a sound FTS5 lexical backend regardless of
what the "correct" BM25 score of any individual query is:

  MR1 — case insensitivity: search("apple") ≡ search("APPLE") ≡
        search("Apple") over the same corpus. Same segment order, same
        BM25 score per hit.

  MR2 — whitespace normalization: search("apple cherry") ≡
        search("apple  cherry") ≡ search("apple\tcherry"). Excess
        spacing must be absorbed by the tokenizer, not folded into
        the BM25 denominator.

  MR3 — idempotence (the "add-then-remove = noop on lexical score"
        shape, restricted to what the public surface exposes): calling
        search(Q) three times back-to-back with no intervening write
        must return identical (segment_id, score) lists. Any FTS5
        mutation across reads breaks this.

  MR4 — no-op prune is identity: prune_segments_before(0) removes zero
        rows (every seeded segment has captured_at > 0) and must leave
        every BM25 score bit-identical before/after. Catches spurious
        delete+reindex side effects.

Canonicalization strategy: `canon()` projects each SearchResult to
(segment.id, score rounded to 1e-6) preserving engine return order.
Snippets are disabled via SearchOptions.include_snippets = Some(false)
because highlighted snippets depend on the query text itself (the
marker around "apple" vs "APPLE" differs), which would break MR1 on
surface equality even when the underlying FTS5 score is identical.

Corpus is deliberately mixed-case with repeated tokens so BM25 has
non-trivial scoring across all four queries.

Verified: 4/4 pass under `--no-default-features --features
mcp,asupersync-runtime` at /tmp/ft-pane6-target in 0.06s wall after
one 38s cold build.
